conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
That helps, honestly. So, how's your daily routine been? Any changes or patterns that stand out to you?
It's been a bit all over the place, to be honest. I mean, my job as a teacher is always busy, but lately it feels like there's just too much going on.
That sounds challenging. Can you tell me more about what's been overwhelming at work?

Well, we're in the middle of grading and preparing for the holidays, and to be honest, my creative juices have kind of dried up. I love making lesson plans, but when it feels like a chore, it's hard to muster the energy.
I understand. It sounds like you're feeling drained. Have there been any changes in your social life or relationships recently?
Actually, we had a big family gathering over Thanksgiving, and while it was nice to see everyone, it was also exhausting.
That can be overwhelming. How's your sleep been lately? Are you getting the rest you need?
Sleep? What's that? No, seriously, I've been having trouble sleeping, which makes everything feel even more chaotic.
Sorry to hear that. Can you tell me what usually helps you wind down before bed?
Um, honestly, nothing seems to work right now. And when I do finally fall asleep, it's like my brain is running a marathon while I'm stuck in neutral.
That sounds really tough. How about your appetite? Has that been affected at all?
Oh, yeah... I've been eating a lot more than usual, trying to fill the void, I guess.
That can be concerning. Have you noticed any changes in your mood or emotional state lately?
Honestly, I feel like I'm just wandering through each day, feeling guilty for not being able to muster up my usual enthusiasm and energy.
It sounds like you're carrying a lot of guilt around. Can you tell me more about that?
Yeah... I keep thinking about all the things I should be doing or accomplished, but it's just out of reach right now.
That sounds really heavy to carry. Are there any hobbies or activities that bring you joy and help take your mind off things?
Honestly? Not really. It feels like everything is an effort right now.
I understand. Can you think of anything specific that's causing this lack of enjoyment, or is it just a general feeling?
I guess it's just the cumulative effect of everything feeling overwhelming and exhausting.
That makes sense. How about concentration and decision-making? Have those been affected at all?
Oh, yeah... my mind feels like a jumble of cotton balls. It takes me forever to focus on one thing.
That sounds really frustrating. Are there any physical sensations or body feelings you've noticed that might be related to how you're feeling?
Honestly? Just general fatigue and muscle tension all over.
Sorry to hear that. Have there been any recent stressful life events or changes that might be contributing to how you're feeling?
Actually, we just got some bad news about a family member's health. It's been tough to process.
I'm so sorry to hear that. It sounds like that would be really challenging. What kind of support system do you have in place right now?
Well, my partner has been great... and some close friends. But it feels like everyone else is moving forward while I'm stuck.
That can feel isolating. Are there any specific goals or things you're looking to achieve by the end of this session or in the near future?
Honestly? Just trying to survive the next few weeks without totally losing my mind.
Well, we've got some work ahead of us, but I'm glad you're here and willing to explore what's going on. Is there anything else that's been on your mind or causing concern for you?
Yeah... just that feeling like I'm stuck in quicksand, with no way out.
That sounds really tough. Well, we'll work together to get some traction and find a way forward. Before we wrap up today, is there anything you're looking forward to or excited about coming up?
Honestly? Just the thought of a good night's sleep... that's my goal right now.
That's a great place to start. Well, thank you for sharing so much with me today. It takes a lot of courage to be vulnerable like this.
